commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Prometheus values indentation bug and Thanos StatefulSet drift

The thanosService insertion misindented storageSpec, scrape configs,
and selector overrides under thanosService instead of prometheusSpec.
This caused Prometheus to lose persistent storage, annotation-based
pod scraping, and label-agnostic ServiceMonitor selection.

Also adds explicit updateStrategy, apiVersion, and kind to Thanos
StatefulSet VCTs to prevent ArgoCD sync drift.

commit.message:
fix: add caching and DB write-back for GBP canonical location ID

- Add module-level in-memory cache (1h TTL) to resolveCanonicalLocationId
  so repeated calls within the same server process skip API calls entirely
- resolveCanonicalLocationId now throws (RATE_LIMITED: prefix or descriptive
  error) instead of silently returning the wrong Place ID on failure
- Track hitRateLimit across all resolution attempts to surface the correct
  error type to callers
- syncGbpReviews and postReply now resolve canonical ID once, write it back
  to social_connections.metadata.locationId (best-effort, fire-and-forget)
  so subsequent syncs skip resolution entirely
- Cron route also resolves and writes back canonical ID per account; rate-
  limit errors are logged as warnings and processing continues to next account
- Remove all three quota/429 fallback blocks in resolveGoogleLocation that
  stored the raw Place ID as locationId — these caused the Reviews API 404
  to repeat on every sync indefinitely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: make cookie refresh a local-only script with expiry detection

Twitter blocks login from datacenter IPs, so automated refresh
from GitHub Actions isn't feasible. Instead:
- Local script: refreshes cookies + updates GitHub secret in one step
- Collector logs clear TWITTER_COOKIES_EXPIRED message when 0 items
- Workflow kept as manual-only fallback (no cron)

auth_token cookies last ~5 years, so refresh is rarely needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix line lookup API returning 'Line not found' for valid lines

The /api/files/:filename/line/:line endpoint was using startOffset to
read a partial file segment, then indexing by global line number into
that segment. This caused line lookups to fail for any line beyond the
segment size. Switch to streaming readline to find the target line
without loading the entire file.

Change-Id: I4507006f1601ee777e5de45d6e31636a3cc60135
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
optimize alignment and buffer allocation

- fix opt_set_mat: remove incorrectly placed mat[k++] = 0

commit.message:
Add Module System v2 with deep nesting, aliases, visibility, and stdlib extensions

- Recursive sub-namespace scanning (arbitrary depth)
- Import aliases (import pkg as m, import pkg.sub as s)
- Diamond dependency deduplication via loaded_names HashSet
- Visibility enforcement (fn / mod fn / local fn) with is_self_import tracking
- flatten_member_chain for arbitrary-depth member access in checker and emitter
- Sub-module import resolution (sub-modules can import other packages)
- 13 new stdlib functions (int.parse/abs/min/max, string.pad_right/trim_start/trim_end/count, list.index_of/last/chunk/sum/product)
- Module system spec at docs/specs/module-system.md
- 25 specification tests in exercises/mod-test/mod_system_test.almd

commit.message:
forge: learn 2 warden rule(s) from PR #131 [no-changelog] (#132)

* forge: learn 2 warden rule(s) from PR #131

* ci: trigger fresh CI run with updated PR title

The changelog-check CI job reads PR_TITLE from the pull_request event
payload. Re-running a failed job reuses the stale payload, so the
[no-changelog] tag added to the title was not picked up. This empty
commit triggers a new workflow run with the current title.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Robin Smith <Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no>

commit.message:
eval: NanoClaw — container-isolated personal AI agent (watch)

Score 9/15 for cortex, 7/15 for manna-ai. Cost model mismatch
(API tokens vs subscription), architecture mismatch (Agent SDK
vs dossier execution), and fork maintenance burden outweigh
benefits for solopreneur setup. Revisit when /loop proves
insufficient or API pricing improves.

block describing exactly what will change and prompt for AGREE before

commit.message:
fix: overhaul resolveCanonicalLocationId with logging and robust extraction

Four agent audit identified three concurrent defects:
- Silent failures: all 8 error paths swallowed with no logging
- Wrong regex: /^locations\/\d+$/ rejects valid account-scoped names
  like accounts/123/locations/456 - now uses extractLocationSegment()
  to pull the locations/{id} portion from any resource name format
- Wrong accounts endpoint: try both Business Information API and
  Account Management API bases so either works

Detailed console.warn at every step so the next failure is diagnosable
from Vercel logs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
